question_text: A maglev train uses electromagnets to levitate above the track. If one electromagnet in the train has 500 coils and produces a lifting force of 8000 N, calculate the approximate lifting force if the number of coils is increased to 750, assuming all other factors stay the same.
correct_answer: 12,000 N (or 12 kN). The lifting force is proportional to the number of coils, so: 8000 N ÷ 500 = 16 N per coil; 16 N × 750 = 12,000 N.
explanation: The strength of an electromagnet increases proportionally with the number of coils. By increasing coils from 500 to 750 (a factor of 1.5), the force also increases by a factor of 1.5: 8000 N × 1.5 = 12,000 N. This is why maglev trains use many coils to generate strong lifting forces.
